buildSteps.label: Think about it: what could you do if you knew where every edge was?
buildSteps.description: Take a moment — where might this be valuable?

buildSteps.label: Self-driving cars
buildSteps.description: Find lane markings, pedestrians, and road signs by detecting their edges first

buildSteps.label: Medical imaging
buildSteps.description: Outline tumors, organs, and fractures in X-rays, MRIs, and CT scans

buildSteps.label: Manufacturing & quality control
buildSteps.description: Inspect products on assembly lines — spot cracks, misalignments, and defects in real time

buildSteps.label: Robotics & navigation
buildSteps.description: Robots use edges to understand room layouts, avoid obstacles, and pick up objects

buildSteps.label: The takeaway
buildSteps.description: A simple 3×3 kernel — the Sobel filter — is the foundation for all of this. Filtering is one of the most important building blocks in computer vision.
